verify_invalid: Report NOT verified: SHA-256 hash does NOT match.
verify_bad_format: The file does not have the expected format of a ForZip report.
verify_report_not_found: The specified report does not exist.
verify_no_sidecar: The integrity file (.sha256) was not found next to the report.
verify_sidecar_read_error: Could not read the integrity file:
verify_sidecar_bad_format: The integrity file (.sha256) does not contain a valid SHA-256 hash.

report_subtitle: Generated by ForZip — Forensic compression and verification tool
report_disclaimer_text:
This report was automatically generated by ForZip, a free and open-source
tool distributed under the Apache License 2.0. Results are provided
"AS IS", without warranties of any kind. It is the sole responsibility
of the operator to validate the suitability of this tool and its results
for forensic use in their jurisdiction. ForZip does not replace the
professional judgment of the examiner.
error_no_algorithms: At least one hash algorithm must be selected.
error_wrong_password: Incorrect password. Make sure it matches the one used during compression.
error_no_char_class: At least one character class must be enabled.
error_password_length: Length must be between 8 and 128 characters.
shell_integration_desc: Adds ForZip to the Explorer right-click menu (Compress, Extract, Verify, Hash). Portable: nothing is installed, no administrator required, and it can be removed at any time.
shell_status_registered: Status: integrated. ForZip appears in the right-click menu.
shell_status_unsupported: Context menu integration is only available on Windows.
shell_status_stale: Warning: the executable was moved. Re-integrate to update the menu path.
confirm_operator_msg:
This information will be included in the forensic report.
Please verify it is correct before proceeding.
simple_mode_hint: Drag your evidence, review and package. ForZip applies the recommended forensic defaults: SHA-256 fingerprint of every file, forensic report and verification manifest.
result_subtitle: The files below were generated. Keep them together: they form the evidence package. If you move or send the evidence, include all of them.
result_zip_desc: The package containing your evidence. This is the file to preserve.
result_volumes_desc: The package was split into volumes: ALL of them are required to open or verify it.
result_report_desc: Human-readable forensic report: who packaged, when, and the fingerprint of every file. Ideal for printing or attaching to a case file.
result_manifest_desc: Technical manifest that allows automatic verification of the evidence. Do not edit it or separate it from the ZIP.
result_sidecar_desc: SHA-256 fingerprint of the report: detects whether the report was modified.
result_signature_desc: Digital signature of the manifest: cryptographic proof of who signed it.
result_signature_ts_desc: Digital signature of the manifest with an RFC 3161 timestamp: authorship and date verifiable by third parties.
result_no_report: The forensic report was not generated (the operator dialog was cancelled). The ZIP exists anyway, but without a report the evidence cannot be verified later.
result_timestamp_failed: The signature was created, but the timestamp server did not respond: the signing date is declared by the signer (not verifiable by third parties).
log_shell_action_error: Could not process the context-menu action: {0}
log_shell_repaired: Context menu repaired automatically: the registered path no longer existed.
log_shell_integrated: ForZip is now integrated into the Explorer right-click menu.
log_operator_confirm: Requesting operator confirmation for the report...
log_report_skipped: The operator dialog was cancelled. The report was not generated.
log_hash_copied: Package SHA-256 hash copied to clipboard.
log_open_folder_error: Could not open the results folder: {0}
log_extract_cancelled: Extraction cancelled by the user. Cleaning up partial files...
log_hash_cancelled: Hash calculation cancelled by the user.
log_report_export_cancelled: Report export cancelled by the user.
verify_ts_corrupt: RFC 3161 timestamp: present but unreadable or corrupt.
verify_ts_absent: RFC 3161 timestamp: absent (date declared by the signer).
verify_content_error_hint: (missing password, or corrupt archive?) Per-volume verification was still performed.
verify_external_ok: Successful verification via external hash file (.sha256).
shell_offer_title: Add ForZip to the right-click menu?
shell_offer_msg:
ForZip can integrate with the Windows Explorer context menu: Compress, Extract, Verify and Hash one click away.

Nothing is installed, no administrator rights are required, and you can remove it anytime from Settings → Integration.
timestamp_hint: An external authority (TSA) certifies the date and time of the signature, making it verifiable by third parties. Requires an internet connection.
